title: Seismic Data Analysis
intro: This application loads and processes seismic records from various data formats. Use the Analysis menu to switch between modules. Click Run to execute the current analysis.

sections.heading: Data
sections.summary: Load seismic record files and inspect channel metadata.
steps: Click the File button in the header toolbar.
Select one or more files. Supported formats: .vif, .txt, .v1, .raw, .msd, .asc, .dat.
Loaded channels appear in the Files & Channels sidebar tree on the left.
The channel table shows File Name, Channel number, Record Length, Sampling rate, Signal Type, Unit, Scale factor, Date & Time, and Azimuth for each channel.
Click a channel in the sidebar or table to select it for analysis.
Click Download in the header to export the channel table to Excel.
notes: Multiple files can be loaded at the same time. Each file may contain one or more channels.
Right-clicking in the sidebar opens a context menu with options to select channels by sampling rate, channel type, or azimuth orientation.

sections.heading: Filter
sections.summary: Apply baseline correction and digital filtering to the selected channel.

parameters.label: Baseline Correction
parameters.description: Removes the DC offset or trend from the record before filtering. Remove Mean subtracts the signal mean. Linear, Quadratic, and Qubic fit and subtract a polynomial trend.

parameters.label: Filter Type
parameters.description: Butterworth: maximally flat magnitude response in the passband. Chebyshev: steeper roll-off at the cost of passband ripple — set the Maximum Ripple Size accordingly. Bessel: maximally flat group delay, preserving pulse shape.

parameters.label: Filter Band
parameters.description: Low-Pass: retains frequencies below F1. High-Pass: retains frequencies above F1. Band-Pass: retains frequencies between F1 and F2.

parameters.label: Filter Order
parameters.description: Controls the steepness of the roll-off. Higher order = sharper cutoff, but greater phase distortion (unless Zero Phase is enabled) and potential numerical instability at high orders.

parameters.label: Cut-off Frequency F1 (Hz)
parameters.description: Lower cut-off frequency. Used as the sole cut-off for Low-Pass and High-Pass filters, and as the lower bound for Band-Pass.

parameters.label: Cut-off Frequency F2 (Hz)
parameters.description: Upper cut-off frequency. Used only for Band-Pass filtering as the upper bound.

parameters.label: Maximum Ripple Size (dB)
parameters.description: Applies to the Chebyshev filter only. Defines the maximum allowable passband ripple in decibels. Larger values produce a steeper roll-off.

parameters.label: Zero Phase Filtering
parameters.description: Applies the filter twice — once forward and once backward — to eliminate phase distortion. This doubles the effective filter order. Recommended for all seismic processing.

notes: F2 is only active when Band-Pass is selected. It is greyed out otherwise.
The Ripple Size parameter is only active when Chebyshev is selected.
Zero Phase Filtering is strongly recommended. Disabling it will introduce a time-shift in the filtered output.

sections.heading: Integral
sections.summary: Numerically integrate the filtered signal to obtain velocity and displacement time histories.
steps: Select the channels to display using the toggle switches.
Click Run to compute the integrals and update the graph.

parameters.label: Original Signal
parameters.description: Display the original (baseline-corrected and filtered) signal.

parameters.label: 1st Integral
parameters.description: Display the first numerical integral of the signal (e.g. velocity if the input is acceleration).

parameters.label: 2nd Integral
parameters.description: Display the second numerical integral of the signal (e.g. displacement if the input is acceleration).

notes: Integration is performed using the trapezoidal rule after baseline correction and filtering.
Baseline drift in the integrated signal can indicate insufficient low-frequency filtering. Try increasing the high-pass cut-off frequency in the Filter module.

sections.heading: SDOF
sections.summary: Compute the dynamic response of a Single Degree of Freedom (SDOF) oscillator subjected to the selected ground motion record.

parameters.label: Analysis Type
parameters.description: Free Vibration: response to initial conditions only, no external excitation. Forced Vibration: steady-state response to harmonic excitation. Piece-Wise Exact: exact solution assuming linearly interpolated excitation between time steps. Central Difference: explicit finite difference method — conditionally stable, requires small time steps. Newmark Linear: unconditionally stable implicit method for linear systems (α=0.5, β=0.25). Newmark Non-Linear: iterative Newmark method for inelastic systems with hysteretic behavior.

parameters.label: Select to display
parameters.description: Choose which response quantity to display on the graph (e.g. displacement, velocity, acceleration, force).

parameters.label: Frequency (Hz)
parameters.description: Natural frequency of the SDOF oscillator.

parameters.label: Damping Ratio
parameters.description: Viscous damping ratio ξ (dimensionless). A value of 0.05 corresponds to 5% critical damping, typical for reinforced concrete structures.

parameters.label: Sampling Interval (s)
parameters.description: Time step Δt used for the numerical integration. Should be small relative to the natural period T. See the dt/T limit in Settings.

parameters.label: Record Duration (s)
parameters.description: Total duration of the output time history.

parameters.label: Initial Displacement
parameters.description: Initial displacement of the SDOF mass at t = 0.

parameters.label: Initial Velocity
parameters.description: Initial velocity of the SDOF mass at t = 0.

parameters.label: Harmonic Excitation Amplitude
parameters.description: Amplitude of the harmonic forcing function. Used only when Analysis Type is set to Forced Vibration.

parameters.label: Harmonic Excitation Frequency (Hz)
parameters.description: Frequency of the harmonic forcing function. Set this equal to the natural frequency to simulate resonance. Used only for Forced Vibration.

parameters.label: Yield Force
parameters.description: Normalized yield force (force / weight) for the inelastic SDOF. Used only for Newmark Non-Linear analysis.

parameters.label: Post-Yield Hardening Ratio
parameters.description: Ratio of the post-yield stiffness to the initial elastic stiffness (α). A value of 0 corresponds to an elastic-perfectly-plastic model.

parameters.label: Unloading Stiffness Degradation
parameters.description: Controls the reduction in unloading stiffness as ductility demand increases (Clough degrading stiffness model). A value of 0 gives no degradation (bilinear model).

notes: For Newmark Non-Linear, convergence tolerances and integration coefficients can be adjusted in the Settings module.
The Central Difference method is conditionally stable: the time step must satisfy Δt ≤ T/π, where T is the natural period.

sections.heading: Response Spectrum
sections.summary: Compute elastic or constant-ductility inelastic response spectra from the selected ground motion record.

parameters.label: Spectrum Type
parameters.description: Elastic Spectra: computes Sd (spectral displacement), Sv (pseudo-velocity), and Sa (pseudo-acceleration) for linear SDOF oscillators across the period range. Constant-Ductility Inelastic Spectra: iterates to find the yield strength reduction factor R that produces exactly the target ductility μ for each period, using either the bilinear or Clough degrading stiffness hysteretic model.

parameters.label: Select to display
parameters.description: Choose which spectral quantity to display (Sa, Sv, Sd, or inelastic equivalents).

parameters.label: Minimum Period (s)
parameters.description: Start of the period range for spectral computation.

parameters.label: Period Step (s)
parameters.description: Increment between successive periods. Smaller values produce a smoother spectrum but increase computation time.

parameters.label: Maximum Period (s)
parameters.description: End of the period range for spectral computation.

parameters.label: Unloading Stiffness Degradation
parameters.description: Used for the Clough inelastic model. Controls degradation of unloading stiffness with increasing ductility demand.

parameters.label: Post-Yield Hardening Ratio
parameters.description: Ratio of post-yield to initial stiffness for the bilinear hysteretic model.

parameters.label: Damping Ratio Count
parameters.description: Number of damping ratio values to include. Up to 4 curves can be plotted simultaneously (ξ₁ through ξ₄).

parameters.label: Damping Ratios ξ₁ – ξ₄
parameters.description: Viscous damping ratio values for each spectrum curve. Common values: 0.02 (steel), 0.05 (reinforced concrete), 0.10 (heavily damped structures).

parameters.label: Ductility Count
parameters.description: Number of target ductility values for inelastic spectra. Up to 4 curves (μ₁ through μ₄).

parameters.label: Constant Ductility μ₁ – μ₄
parameters.description: Target displacement ductility ratios for constant-ductility inelastic spectra. μ = 1 corresponds to the elastic spectrum.

notes: Inelastic spectral computation is iterative and significantly more computationally intensive than elastic spectra. Reduce the period range or increase the period step if computation is slow.
The Damping Ratio Count and Ductility Count fields control how many of the corresponding input rows are active.

sections.heading: Spectrum
sections.summary: Compute frequency-domain representations of the selected channel.

parameters.label: Select to display
parameters.description: Fourier Amplitude Spectrum: magnitude of the FFT of the signal. Power Spectrum: squared magnitude of the FFT, proportional to signal energy. Spectral Density Spectrum (PSD): average power per unit frequency, estimated using Welch's method. Spectrogram: time-varying frequency content using a sliding window STFT.

parameters.label: Window Length (s)
parameters.description: Length of each segment used in Welch's PSD estimate and the Spectrogram. Longer windows give better frequency resolution but poorer time resolution.

parameters.label: Overlap Ratio
parameters.description: Fraction of overlap between successive windows for the PSD and Spectrogram. Higher overlap (e.g. 0.75) smooths the estimate.

parameters.label: Smoothing Window Type
parameters.description: Hamming: reduces spectral leakage from signal edges — recommended for most seismic signals. Rectangular: no tapering applied; highest frequency resolution but significant leakage.

notes: Window Length and Overlap Ratio apply to the Spectral Density Spectrum and Spectrogram only. They have no effect on the Fourier Amplitude or Power Spectrum.
The predominant period of a record can be identified from the peak of the Fourier Amplitude Spectrum.

sections.heading: Strong Motion Parameters
sections.summary: Compute intensity measures and strong motion parameters from the selected record.

parameters.label: Select to display
parameters.description: Choose which strong motion parameter table or plot to display.

parameters.label: Bracketed Duration Threshold (g)
parameters.description: Acceleration threshold used to define the Bracketed Duration — the time between the first and last exceedance of this level. The default of 0.05g is standard per Bolt (1969).

notes: Computed parameters typically include: PGA, PGV, PGD, Arias Intensity, CAV, Housner Intensity, Bracketed Duration, Significant Duration (5–95% Arias), and RMS acceleration.
All parameters are computed on the baseline-corrected and filtered signal.

sections.heading: Drift
sections.summary: Compute inter-story drift from displacement records or by double-integrating acceleration channels.
notes: Ensure baseline correction and high-pass filtering are applied before computing drift from integrated acceleration records — low-frequency noise accumulates through integration and will dominate the displacement signal.

sections.heading: H/V Spectral Ratio
sections.summary: Compute the Horizontal-to-Vertical spectral ratio (HVSR / Nakamura technique) from three-component recordings.
notes: Requires at least one horizontal and one vertical channel to be selected.
The peak of the H/V curve approximates the fundamental resonance frequency of the site.

sections.heading: P-Wave
sections.summary: Detect the P-wave arrival time from the selected channel.
notes: P-wave picking can be used to determine the onset of strong motion and to compute S-P time differences for epicentral distance estimation.

sections.heading: Settings
sections.summary: Configure numerical integration parameters and output units for graphs.

parameters.label: Alpha (α)
parameters.description: Newmark velocity parameter. α = 0.5 gives no artificial damping (average acceleration method).

parameters.label: Beta (β)
parameters.description: Newmark displacement parameter. β = 0.25 with α = 0.5 gives the unconditionally stable constant average acceleration method.

parameters.label: Maximum Δt/T
parameters.description: Maximum allowable ratio of the integration time step to the natural period. If the record sampling interval exceeds this limit, the analysis will sub-step automatically.

parameters.label: Out-of-balance Force Tolerance
parameters.description: Convergence tolerance on the residual (out-of-balance) force for the non-linear Newmark iteration.

parameters.label: Ductility Convergence Tolerance
parameters.description: Convergence tolerance on the ductility demand for constant-ductility inelastic spectra computation.
